name: Leek Potato Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel potatoes in advance and keep submerged under cold water to prevent discoloration.
2. Smash garlic clusters on their sides, separate cloves by cutting off the large end, crush cloves with a knife, remove peelings.
3. Inspect leeks, remove any wilted or discolored parts, chop onions.
4. Coarsely chop leeks including leaves and sauté in peanut oil until tender. Use olive oil or corn oil if needed. Drain excess oil.
5. Select a pot large enough to cook potatoes with room for soup. Add 3 cups chicken stock and 3 cups water. Ensure stock is clear without fat; use instant broth if homemade stock unavailable.
6. Cook potatoes in stock/water combination for 25-30 minutes or until done. Reduce heat, drain potatoes, ensure sautéed vegetables are drained.
7. Place potatoes and drained vegetables in a food processor and blend on medium to high speed until smooth. Return blended mixture to soup pot and stir well.
8. Increase heat to boiling; if thick, add more stock or water. Add salt and pepper to taste.
9. When liquid reaches boiling point, add milk or cream. Stir. Milk makes the soup thinner than cream. Cream or Half-and-Half adds full flavor with body. Heat just below boiling point.
10. Serve immediately with fresh baked bread or homemade croutons.
Note: Ingredient amounts are not critical; more garlic and onions can be added if desired. For no food processor, mash potatoes separately and blend sautéed vegetables before adding to stock.
